name: Rhubarb - Stewed
recipeIngredient: 10-1/2 pounds rhubarb, spring or late fall crop (for 7 quarts)
28-pound lug yields 14 to 28 quarts (average 1-1/2 pounds per quart)
recipeInstructions:
1. Select young, tender, well-colored stalks.
2. Trim off leaves.
3. Wash stalks and cut into 1/2-inch to 1-inch pieces.
4. In a large saucepan, add 1/2 cup sugar for each quart of fruit.
5. Let stand until juice appears.
6. Heat gently to boiling.
7. Fill jars without delay, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
8. Adjust lids and process.
Processing times:
- Boiling-water canner (Table 1):
- Altitudes 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 minutes
- Altitudes 1,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
- Altitude above 6,000 ft: 25 minutes
- Dial-gauge pressure canner (Table 2):
- Altitudes 0 - 2,000 ft: 6 lb PSI
- Altitudes 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 7 lb PSI
- Altitudes 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 8 lb PSI
- Altitude above 6,000 ft: 9 lb PSI
- Weighted-gauge pressure canner (Table 3):
- Altitudes 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 lb PSI
- Altitude above 1,000 ft: 10 lb PSI
